title: attribution — compliance evidence
description: Attribution is privacy-sensitive by design — it processes personal data (behavioural marketing metadata) under GDPR Art. 6(1)(f) legitimate interest, gated by explicit user consent under Art. 6(1)(a) for personalized advertising. Full GDPR + CCPA + LGPD alignment. NO health data, NO financial account data, NO minor-specific data (attribution operates on the User/session level, minors are attributed through their guardian's User row per user::hierarchy). PCI-DSS scope: N/A — attribution captures no card data ever.

controls.evidence: Attribution capture is disclosed in the tenant's privacy policy under the 'marketing tracking' section. AttributionSuppressedByConsent metric proves the consent-gate fires when consent is revoked.

Attribution data is used ONLY for: (1) marketing conversion attribution, (2) product analytics, (3) referral program crediting. Non-goals explicitly list forbidden uses: no third-party sale, no cross-tenant stitching, no cross-application merging, no minor-targeting.

IP addresses stored as SHA-256 hash only (raw IPs never stored). Device parsing extracts browser/OS/device_type — no raw fingerprint. Landing page URL stored but query parameters beyond utm_* are stripped.

Touchpoint retention: 730 days default (2 years); Enterprise 2555 days (7 years) for financial-audit alignment. Reset requests purge touchpoints 90 days post-request (audit hold). Churned profiles archive after 730 days.

Base attribution capture (utm_* + landing_page + referrer + device) operates on legitimate interest for marketing attribution — a fundamental business function. Legitimate interest assessment (LIA) documented per tenant.

Click IDs + personalized advertising features require explicit consent under `marketing` + `advertising` + `personalized_advertising` categories. Consent snapshot captured on every touchpoint for regulator queryability.
Consent revocation flows through the compliance::consent module. AttributionConsentSnapshotUpdated fires when consent changes; future touchpoints refuse persistence past the revocation point.

Users see their own attribution profile via GET /api/v1/me/attribution + full touchpoint history via GET /api/v1/me/attribution/touchpoints. Data-export flow (Wave 5+) surfaces the full history in machine-readable format (JSON/CSV).
Users can request correction via support flow. Admin edits go through attribution:describe + attribution:reset (correction = reset + re-capture).

attribution:reset command implements the right-to-erasure. Profile fields redacted immediately + touchpoints hard-deleted 90 days later (audit hold). Every reset writes to audits with 7-year retention. Force-reset (platform plane) available for regulator-issued requests.
GET /api/v1/me/attribution returns the profile + touchpoints in machine-readable JSON. CSV export available on request via support flow.
Users may object to attribution processing by revoking `marketing` consent. AttributionMiddleware then refuses persistence — profile becomes ephemeral in-memory only.

controls.principle: data protection by design + by default
controls.evidence: IP hashing (SHA-256) is the default — raw IPs cannot be stored. Consent gate runs BEFORE persistence, not after. Non-Enterprise tenants get partial-attribution (safer default than aggressive cross-device merging).
This module's subprocessors.json declares external processors (empty by default). Every processing category (capture / merge / rollup / reset) is documented + auditable.
Same as GDPR Art. 15 — GET /me/attribution surfaces the profile.
Same as GDPR Art. 17 — attribution:reset command redacts + purges.
Attribution data is NEVER sold. Non-goal explicitly documented in readme.md §12 and module.json.
AttributionMiddleware honors the Sec-GPC header — when set to '1', treats the request as consent-withdrawal for `marketing` + `advertising` + `personalized_advertising` categories.
Same lawful basis map as GDPR (legitimate interest + explicit consent).
Same rights implementation as GDPR (access / correction / erasure / portability / objection).
attribution.view-all + attribution.reset + attribution.merge are seeded to admin/owner only. Marketing role sees aggregated rollups without individual PII.
All /api/v1/attributions routes are TLS-only + require auth:sanctum + entitlement middleware. Platform routes additionally require auth:platform_admin.

academorix.attribution.consent_suppressions_total counter tracks consent-gate refusals. academorix.attribution.count_drift_corrections counter tracks integrity drift. Both feed the observability dashboard.

IP hashing prevents raw-IP leakage. Consent gate prevents unauthorised persistence. Reset flow with 90-day audit hold + hard-delete satisfies the right-to-erasure without violating financial-record retention.

pci_dss.reason: Attribution captures NO card data — the module's boundary explicitly excludes payment methods. Financial attribution (which subscription tier a subject purchased) is captured downstream by finance module (Wave 5) via the frozen attribution snapshot on invoice/subscription rows.

controls.evidence: Attribution SDUI screens use HeroUI's design tokens which meet AA contrast by default. Lifecycle-state chip colours (anonymous / identified / converted / churned) do not rely on colour alone — each carries a text label + icon.
Attribution timeline widget + merge dialog are fully keyboard-navigable per HeroUI Table + Dialog semantics.
Reset confirmation dialog uses aria-live=polite on the confirmation message. Merge-progress banner uses aria-live=polite for the async job status.
